overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgement may waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchisees retain full ability to bring fraud-in-the-inducement claims regardless of any representations in the franchise agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Any release required for renewal or transfer does not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees cannot be forced to release claims under Maryland franchise law as a condition of renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law; franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Insolvency-based termination may be limited by bankruptcy law in Maryland.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law, subject to arbitration obligations, unless preempted by FAA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees may litigate Maryland franchise law claims in Maryland rather than Illinois.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland franchise law, to extent required.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchise law governs Maryland-specific claims, overriding Illinois choice of law.

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland franchise law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Statute of limitations specific to Maryland franchise claims.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause. Good cause includes failure to comply with lawful provisions with written notice and 30-day cure opportunity.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan imposes a good-cause termination requirement that limits franchisor's ability to terminate.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to renew without fairly compensating franchisee for fair market value of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, and furnishings (if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under other trademark, or <6 months notice).
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan requires fair-value compensation on non-renewal under certain conditions.

overrides.override_summary: Michigan prohibits requiring arbitration or litigation outside the state. However, franchisor intends to enforce arbitration provision regardless.
overrides.why_it_matters: Legal uncertainty: franchisor explicitly states it considers Michigan's out-of-state arbitration prohibition unconstitutional and will enforce arbitration as written.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require franchisee to assent to release, assignment, novation, waiver, or estoppel depriving franchisee of rights under Michigan Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Releases that waive Michigan franchise law protections are void.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days' notice for non-renewal, except in specified cases under Minn. Stat. §80C.14.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice and cure periods than the standard franchise agreement provides.

overrides.override_summary: Any release for renewal/transfer does not apply to claims arising under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain franchise law claims even when signing general releases.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota. Franchisee retains right to submit matters to Minnesota courts and any procedure, forum, or remedies provided by Minnesota law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees can litigate in Minnesota despite Illinois forum clause.

overrides.override_summary: Prohibits waiver of jury trial, waiver of procedures/remedies, and consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes. However, franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions in the franchise agreement may be unenforceable in Minnesota.

overrides.override_summary: Commissioner has determined termination or liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust, and inequitable under Section 51-19-09. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota may not enforce liquidated damages or termination damages provisions.

overrides.override_summary: Any release will not apply to claims arising under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees retain franchise law claims regardless of releases.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site (not Chicago) to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law. Franchisee may bring action in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may not be forced to arbitrate in Chicago.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to extent required by law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice of law for North Dakota franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of punitive damages (Section 14.5) may be deleted if required by ND Franchise Investment Law. Time limitations may be modified.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may retain right to punitive damages and different statute of limitations.

overrides.override_summary: Litigation in home state except as required by law for claims under Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchisees may litigate RI franchise law claims in Rhode Island.

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law generally applies except as required by law for claims under Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchise law governs RI-specific claims.
